payload.text:
Create a complete UX flow and high-fidelity UI mockups for a product called AutoCreator Studio, an AI-powered short-form video creation platform for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

The product automatically turns ideas into viral short-form videos: scripts, hooks, captions, visuals, voiceovers, posting schedules, and performance optimization.

Design Philosophy:
– Creator-first
– Speed + clarity
– Minimal but expressive
– Dark mode primary
– One strong accent color (neon blue or neon purple)
– Fast, modular, confident

1. Welcome & Onboarding

Screen 1 — Welcome
– Bold headline: “Create viral short-form content — automatically.”
– Subtext: Scripts. Hooks. Captions. Videos. Done.
– CTA: Start Creating

Screen 2 — Platform Selection
– Select platforms: TikTok, Reels, Shorts
– Toggle cards with platform icons

Screen 3 — Creator Type
– Options:
– Personal Brand
– Business
– Coach
– Influencer
– Faceless Channel

Screen 4 — Niche Selection
– Chip-style tags:
– AI
– Finance
– Fitness
– Motivation
– Education
– Ecommerce

2. Home Dashboard (Creator Command Center)

Dashboard Layout
– Top section: “Today’s Content”
– Content pipeline progress bar
– Quick Actions:
– Generate Hooks
– Create Video
– Write Captions
– Schedule Posts

Performance Snapshot
– Views
– Engagement
– Growth trend

3. Hook & Idea Generator (Core Differentiator)

Hook Generator Screen
– Input: “What is this video about?”
– AI generates:
– Scroll-stopping hooks
– Curiosity hooks
– Emotional hooks

Hook Cards
– Each card includes:
– Hook text
– Estimated virality score
– Buttons: Save • Rewrite • Use

4. Script Builder

Script Flow
– Structure:
– Hook (0–3 sec)
– Core message
– Call to action

UI
– Clean vertical script layout
– Highlight timing markers
– Buttons:
– Shorten
– Add emotion
– Make it viral

5. Video Generator

Video Creation Screen
– Choose format:
– Talking head
– Faceless (stock + captions)
– Slides + motion

Controls
– AI voice selector
– Caption style selector
– Background video selector

Preview Panel
– Vertical phone preview
– Timeline scrubber

6. Caption & Hashtag Engine

Caption Generator
– Generates:
– Short captions
– Story captions
– CTA captions

Hashtag Panel
– Trending hashtags
– Reach-optimized hashtags
– Niche-specific hashtags

7. Content Calendar & Scheduler

Calendar View
– Weekly / Monthly toggle
– Drag-and-drop posts

Each Post Card
– Platform
– Caption preview
– Video status
– Scheduled time

8. AI Growth Coach

Coach Dashboard
– Daily recommendations:
– Best time to post
– Content type to repeat
– What’s underperforming

Chat Interface
– “Why did this video flop?”
– “What should I post tomorrow?”

9. Analytics & Optimization

Analytics Screen
– Views
– Watch time
– Retention curve
– Hook performance

AI Insights
– “Your hooks work better with questions”
– “Post 2 hours earlier for higher reach”

10. Settings

– Connected accounts
– Brand presets (fonts, colors, caption tone)
– Subscription
– Export options

Visual System

– Dark charcoal background
– Neon accent for action & AI elements
– Rounded modular cards
– Fast animations
– Creator-centric typography
– Clear hierarchy

The experience should feel like a professional creator’s control room — fast, automated, and addictive to use.

**Designing the Onboarding Flow**

I've been meticulously crafting the onboarding sequence for "AutoCreator Studio." I'm focusing on a streamlined yet comprehensive experience, allowing new users to quickly grasp the platform's core functionalities. Specifically, I'm prioritizing clear and concise steps for welcome, platform selection, and creator type identification. I want it to feel intuitive and engaging, not a chore.


**Outlining the Core Features**

I'm now concretizing the feature set for "AutoCreator Studio." I've got the foundational structure figured out: the initial onboarding flow followed by a robust home dashboard with quick actions. Key tools like the Hook & Idea Generator, Script Builder, and Video Generator are taking shape. I'm prioritizing modular design and a clear separation of concerns for both front-end and back-end aspects of the application. The tech stack will now be React 18+ with TypeScript.


**Establishing the Application Structure**

I'm solidifying the application architecture. The `App` component will handle the top-level navigation, with a sidebar and main content. UI elements are taking shape with specific color codes: dark charcoal background, card backgrounds, and a neon purple accent. I've broken down the steps of the initial onboarding sequence and the core features. Step 1 is Onboarding. After that we'll have a Dashboard, Hook Generator, Script Builder, Video Generator, and Growth Coach.

**Developing AI Integration**

I'm currently focused on how to best integrate several advanced AI features into the existing AutoCreator Studio. Specifically, I'm exploring an AI-powered short-form video creation platform and initial ideas are starting to take shape. I'm prioritizing incorporating text prompts for image editing using Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. I'm also considering a live API integration (Native) for enhanced functionality.


**Incorporating New AI**

I've been thinking about the new features. I'm focusing on the integration of real-time voice interaction with Gemini and the Veo video generation. I'm prioritizing adding search grounding to provide current information and incorporating Gemini 3 Pro for higher quality image generation, size/aspect ratio controls, analysis, and a chatbot interface. The architecture for these features is becoming clearer.


**Expanding Feature Scope**

I'm now zeroing in on integrating advanced content analysis, initially within the Growth Coach or Dashboard, to allow users to glean deep AI feedback from uploaded videos and images. My focus shifts towards replacing the placeholder video generator with a full Veo integration. This also includes the implementation of Pro Image Generation to facilitate asset creation, alongside aspect ratio and size controls within the Video Generator's faceless mode. Finally, I'm working to add live API for a real-time brainstorming experience.


**Finalizing Integration Details**

I'm presently working on implementing the finer details. I'm focusing on the integration of aspect ratio and size configuration for image generation and Veo video generation. I've also added an "Analyze Media" button to the Dashboard, and expanded the Video Generator to include image generation and Veo capabilities. The Hook Generator now supports search grounding, and the Growth Coach is upgraded for real-time thinking and voice chat. I've initialized GoogleGenAI, added API key checks, and managed MIME types and PCM decoding for the Live API. I'm updating geminiService.ts with specific calls, and updating UI components to reflect the new features.


**Reorganizing the Interface**

I'm now organizing the core AI features around the Video Generator, reorganizing it into three clear tabs: Generate Visuals using Gemini 3 Pro Image, Animate with Veo, and Edit with Gemini 2.5 Flash Image. I'm also refining the Growth Coach into a conversational chat interface with Gemini 3 Pro and Live API voice features. I've also clarified the model usage.

payload.text:
Design a high-conversion landing page UI for Auto-Creator Studio, an AI-powered TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts creation platform that automatically turns ideas into viral short-form videos.

Auto-Creator Studio generates hooks, scripts, captions, voiceovers, videos, posting schedules, and growth insights — all in one place.

Primary Message:
Create viral short-form content — automatically.

Design Style:
– Creator-first
– Dark mode primary
– One bold accent color (neon blue OR neon purple)
– High contrast
– Minimal but expressive
– Fast, modern, confident
– Clean typography (Inter / SF Pro / Plus Jakarta Sans)
– Subtle motion accents (glows, pulses)

SECTION 1 — HERO

– Large bold headline:
Create viral short-form content — automatically.
– Subheadline:
Hooks. Scripts. Videos. Captions. Scheduled.
– Primary CTA: Start Creating Free
– Secondary CTA: Watch Demo
– Centered product mockup (vertical phone) showing:
– Hook generator
– Script builder
– Video preview
– Background: subtle animated gradient or abstract wave

SECTION 2 — TRUST SIGNAL

– Platform icons: TikTok, Instagram, YouTube
– Caption: Built for creators, brands, and agencies.

SECTION 3 — HOW IT WORKS (3 STEPS)

Step 1 — Generate Hooks
Scroll-stopping hooks in seconds

Step 2 — Auto-Create Videos
Scripts, captions, visuals, voiceovers

Step 3 — Schedule & Grow
Post at the best time, optimize performance

– Each step displayed as a glowing card with icon

SECTION 4 — FEATURE GRID

Display 6 feature cards:

– AI Hook Generator
– Script Builder (viral structure)
– Video Generator (faceless + talking head)
– Caption & Hashtag Engine
– Content Calendar & Scheduler
– AI Growth Coach

– Cards should feel modular, fast, and bold

SECTION 5 — PRODUCT WALKTHROUGH

Split layout:
– Left: vertical scrolling product mockups
– Right: short feature explanations

Sections:
– “Hooks that stop the scroll”
– “Scripts that hold attention”
– “Videos built in minutes”
– “Post at the perfect time”

SECTION 6 — CONTENT PIPELINE VISUAL

Show a horizontal flow:
Idea → Hook → Script → Video → Caption → Schedule → Growth

– Minimal icons
– Neon connector lines

SECTION 7 — TESTIMONIALS

– Card-based creator testimonials
– Metrics-driven quotes:
“My views doubled in 14 days.”
“I post daily without editing anymore.”

SECTION 8 — PRICING

Three-tier pricing:

Starter
Solo creators

Pro (Most Popular)
Daily content + automation

Agency
Teams & multiple brands

– Highlight Pro tier
– Dark cards with glow on hover

SECTION 9 — FINAL CTA

– Big headline:
Your content engine starts here.
– CTA button: Create My First Video
– Minimal, bold, centered

SECTION 10 — FOOTER

– Logo
– Links
– Social icons
– Dark minimal layout

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS

– Desktop + mobile versions
– High-fidelity UI
– Modern SaaS quality
– Clear conversion hierarchy
– Strong creator appeal

The page should feel like a creator’s unfair advantage.

**Refining the Core Message**

I'm now zeroing in on the essence of "AutoCreator Studio". It's all about making viral short-form content effortless, especially for platforms like TikTok and Reels. "Create viral short-form content — automatically" is the driving thought, and it's taking form with a creator-centric, dark mode aesthetic in mind.


**Defining the UI Blueprint**

I'm solidifying the UI structure for the landing page. It'll feature a bold, modern design with Neon Purple accents, high contrast, and the Inter font. The layout's taking shape, including sections for the Hero, Trust Signals, How It Works (3 steps), a Feature Grid (6 cards), a Product Walkthrough, Pipeline Visual, Testimonials, Pricing (3 tiers), a Final CTA, and a Footer. I've also planned `index.html` and `App.tsx` files and considered how to work with the AI service. Now, I will reorganize the entry point so landing page is first.


**Mapping the Landing Structure**

I'm now fully immersed in the layout specifics. The visual walkthrough is taking shape with a sticky image and text arrangement, mirroring a single-page flow. For pricing, a classic three-column approach feels most intuitive. I'm focusing on reorganizing the `App.tsx` file for a `landing` vs. `app` state, which will enable a seamless transition. Also, I have planned out all sections, from the hero to the footer. Now I will update it to handle the button interaction.


**Integrating the App's Core**

I'm now pivoting towards the application's core functionality. After establishing the landing page, the natural next step is transitioning to the onboarding and dashboard flow. I'm visualizing a seamless user experience, with a focus on dark mode aesthetics, glassmorphic cards, and bold Inter typography. Deep blacks and Neon Purple will be the dominant colors, with the intention of being visually arresting and inviting.

I will now design and implement the high-fidelity landing page for AutoCreator Studio. 

### Specification

1.  **Architecture**: I will introduce a `view` state in `App.tsx` to toggle between the `landing` page and the `dashboard` application.
2.  **Visual Language**:
    *   **Palette**: Deep obsidian background (`#0A0A0B`) with Neon Purple (`#A855F7`) accents.
    *   **Typography**: Bold Inter with tight tracking for headlines to evoke a "premium tech" feel.
    *   **Effects**: Subtle radial gradients behind the hero and pricing cards, glassmorphic borders (`border-[#26262B]`), and neon glows on hover.
3.  **Content Sections**:
    *   **Hero**: Features a massive h1 and a "vertical phone" mockup container using Tailwind's `aspect-[9/16]`.
    *   **How it Works**: 3-step cards with numeric indicators and gradient backgrounds.
    *   **Feature Grid**: A 3x2 modular grid with Lucide icons.
    *   **Pipeline**: A visual flow diagram with neon connector lines using CSS `after` elements or simple DIV borders.
    *   **Pricing**: High-contrast cards with the "Pro" tier highlighted via a purple border and glow.
4.  **Interactivity**: The landing page will be fully responsive. Clicking "Start Creating Free" will trigger the onboarding flow previously developed.

payload.text:
Design a complete onboarding UX flow for ViralFlow, an AI-powered short-form content creation studio that automatically generates hooks, scripts, videos, captions, schedules, and growth insights for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.

The onboarding must feel fast, motivating, and creator-native — users should reach their first generated video in under 60 seconds.

Design Style:
– Dark mode primary
– One bold accent color (neon blue or neon purple)
– High contrast
– Minimal text, strong hierarchy
– Smooth transitions
– Creator-centric tone

ONBOARDING GOAL

Understand what the app does

Personalize content generation

Generate the user’s first viral video immediately

SCREEN-BY-SCREEN UX FLOW
Screen 1 — Welcome

– Logo centered
– Headline:
Create viral short-form content — automatically.
– Subheadline:
Hooks. Scripts. Videos. Posted.
– CTA: Start Creating
– Secondary text: No editing skills required

Screen 2 — Choose Platforms

– Select cards with platform icons:
✓ TikTok
✓ Instagram Reels
✓ YouTube Shorts
– Multi-select allowed
– CTA: Continue

Screen 3 — Creator Type

– Card options:
– Personal Brand
– Business
– Coach
– Influencer
– Faceless Channel
– Each card has a short subtitle
– CTA: Next

Screen 4 — Pick Your Niche

– Chip-style tags (scrollable):
– AI
– Finance
– Fitness
– Motivation
– Education
– Ecommerce
– Option: Custom niche input
– CTA: Generate My First Video

Screen 5 — AI Is Working (Micro-Win)

– Loading animation with progress steps:
✓ Finding viral hooks
✓ Writing script
✓ Designing captions
✓ Selecting visuals
– Text: Building your first viral video…

Screen 6 — First Hook Preview

– Display 3 hook options
– Each hook card shows:
– Hook text
– Estimated virality score
– Buttons: Use, Rewrite

Screen 7 — Video Preview

– Vertical video mockup
– Captions visible
– CTA buttons:
– Edit Script
– Change Style
– Publish Later

Screen 8 — Scheduling Prompt

– Suggested best posting time
– Toggle: Auto-schedule my content
– CTA: Schedule My Video

Screen 9 — Success Moment

– Headline:
Your content engine is live 🚀
– Subtext:
ViralFlow will now create and optimize content daily.
– CTA: Go to Dashboard

OPTIONAL UPGRADE MOMENT (SOFT PAYWALL)

– After first video generation

Design the first-time user dashboard UI for ViralFlow, an AI-powered TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts auto-creator studio.

This dashboard appears immediately after onboarding and must guide new users toward consistent content creation with minimal thinking.

Design Style:
– Dark mode primary
– Minimal layout
– High contrast typography
– One neon accent color
– Fast, confident, creator-native

DASHBOARD GOAL

Show clear value immediately

Encourage the next action (create next video)

Build daily usage habit

LAYOUT STRUCTURE
TOP BAR

– Logo (left)
– Content counter: Videos left this month
– Upgrade button (subtle, not aggressive)

SECTION 1 — PRIMARY ACTION (ABOVE THE FOLD)

Large hero card:

Headline:
Create your next viral video

Subtext:
Optimized for TikTok, Reels & Shorts

Primary CTA:
Generate Video

Secondary option:
Use trending format

This button should be visually dominant.

SECTION 2 — TODAY’S CONTENT PLAN

Card titled:
Today’s Posting Recommendation

Displays:
– Best platform to post today
– Recommended hook style
– Suggested posting time

CTA:
Create today’s video

SECTION 3 — QUICK START TEMPLATES

Horizontal scroll cards:

– Viral hook template
– Faceless video template
– Talking-head script
– Educational breakdown

Each card includes:
– Estimated virality score
– Time to create (e.g. “< 2 min”)

SECTION 4 — RECENT CONTENT

Card list showing:
– Video thumbnail (vertical)
– Platform icons
– Status: Draft / Scheduled / Posted

Quick actions:
– Edit
– Duplicate
– Schedule

SECTION 5 — PROGRESS & MOTIVATION

Card titled:
Your Creator Momentum

Displays:
– Videos created this week
– Posting streak
– Encouraging micro-copy:
“Creators who post daily grow 3× faster.”

Subtle progress bar animation

SECTION 6 — AI GROWTH COACH (TEASER)

Locked feature card:

– Title: AI Growth Coach
– Copy:
See what content will perform best next.
– CTA: Unlock with Pro

EMPTY STATE BEHAVIOR

If no videos yet:
– Show a single bold CTA
– Message:
Your content engine is ready. Let’s create your first video.

MICRO-INTERACTIONS

– Button glow on hover
– Progress animations
– Success toast after video creation

MOBILE PRIORITY

– Vertical-first layout
– Thumb-friendly CTAs
– No clutter

UX PRINCIPLES

– One main action per screen
– No analytics overload
– Momentum over perfection
– Feels like a system, not software

The dashboard should feel like a content machine that’s already running — the user needs to press “generate.

**Crafting the Dashboard UI**

I'm now implementing the mobile-first dashboard UI for ViralFlow. The focus is on a clean, dark mode design with neon purple accents and high contrast. I'm updating `App.tsx` to reflect the specific layout and ensure optimal performance on mobile devices.

I'm checking `types.ts` for potential mock data updates related to the content counter, though I think I can handle this in-component. I'm refining the component layout with the Hero Card, Recommendation Card, Templates scroll, Recent Content list, Momentum Card, and Coach Teaser. I'll also modify the Sidebar to align with the new top bar configuration.


**Refining Component Interactions**

I'm currently working to integrate the Hero Card, Recommendation Card, Templates scroll, Recent Content list, Momentum Card, and Coach Teaser in the `DashboardView`. ---

I have updated the `VideoGeneratorView` in `App.tsx` to include platform-specific presets for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts. The UI now features a grid of icon-led buttons that clearly distinguish each platform, even if they share the same 9:16 aspect ratio, providing a more creator-native experience. I also added a visual badge in the mockup preview to confirm the selected format.

In the Dashboard, enhance the 'Performance Snapshot' section to include a comparison to the previous period (e.g., week-over-week growth). Add a small indicator (up/down arrow with percentage) for views and engagement.

**Developing Comparisons**

I'm working on the `Performance Snapshot` of the `DashboardView`. The goal is to add a week-over-week growth comparison. Currently, I'm figuring out the best way to calculate and display the percentage change. I'm thinking of adding a small arrow indicator for visual clarity.


**Refining Metrics & Display**

I've been examining the `DashboardView` code, specifically the `App.tsx` file, and I'm ready to move forward. The initial plan involves enhancing the current display by introducing a row of metric cards. Each card will showcase a core value, along with an indicator illustrating the trend and percentage change. I'm focusing on "Total Views" and "Engagement Rate" as initial examples, while also planning to leverage `lucide-react` icons such as `TrendingUp` and `TrendingDown` for enhanced visualization.


**Creating a Performance View**

I'm now diving into the `DashboardView` code again, specifically the `App.tsx` file.  I realized there isn't an explicit "Performance Snapshot" section as the user suggested, or a good spot for metrics. This means I'll have to create it. Thinking I will either expand the existing "Progress & Motivation" section, or add a dedicated, new section before "Quick Start Templates", but after the hero.


**Positioning Performance Metrics**

I'm now integrating the "Performance Snapshot" section into the `DashboardView` more concretely. I've decided on a new row of cards to enhance the existing structure. This approach lets me show key data like Views, Engagement, and Followers, along with week-over-week comparisons, in a visually appealing way. It maintains the layout's flow while providing valuable insights.


**Incorporating Trend Icons**

I'm now implementing the `TrendingUp` and `TrendingDown` icons from `lucide-react` for the week-over-week comparisons in the "Performance Snapshot." I've added `TrendingDown` to the imports in `App.tsx` and will use these icons alongside the percentage change numbers for clearer visualization. I'm focusing on "Total Views," "Engagement Rate," and "Follower Growth" in the new section. I've re-examined the code and confirmed `App.tsx` is current.
